mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, it signals a confirmed bottom in the labor market, triggering institutional buying of cyclical equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production confirms that supply chain bottlenecks are clearing, allowing corporate margins to expand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months during a post-shock recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate exceeding 0.5 percent confirms economic acceleration, prompting algorithmic momentum strategies to buy equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative predictive value below 40% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns show no statistical difference from untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reduces recession probabilities, which lowers equity risk premiums and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in months with payroll growth are not statistically distinct from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent creates a disinflationary low-rate environment that expands real yields and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 200 basis points over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during a credit expansion phase directly boost consumer discretionary earnings, triggering immediate institutional positioning and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS rises for two consecutive months during a credit expansion phase.
